scenarios.question: During Piaget's sensorimotor stage, children primarily learn through:
scenarios.rationale: The sensorimotor stage (birth-2 years) is characterized by learning through sensory experiences and motor actions. Infants develop object permanence and begin intentional actions. (Slide 5)

scenarios.question: During Piaget's Sensorimotor stage, children primarily learn through:
scenarios.rationale: The sensorimotor stage (birth-2 years) is characterized by learning through sensory experiences and motor actions. Infants develop object permanence and begin intentional actions. (Slide 5)

options: This only applies to adult patients, not children
This is an outdated concept no longer taught
word phrases by 24 months Loss of achieved language or social skills Important t...
This is not a recognized term in pediatric nursing
scenarios.rationale: No spontaneous two: word phrases by 24 months Loss of achieved language or social skills Important to rule out lead poisoning, hearing deficit, neurological disorders, mu (Slide 8)

scenarios.question: When caring for pediatric patients, the nurse should:
options: Use the same approach as with adult patients
Exclude family members from care decisions
be in line with the child’s physical and cognitive abilities Limit-set
Focus only on the child's physical symptoms
scenarios.rationale: be in line with the child’s physical and cognitive abilities Limit-set. Family-centered, age-appropriate care is essential in pediatrics. (Slide 9)

Use the same approach as with adult patients

Findings opposite to what was described
scenarios.rationale: of Autism Preliminary symptoms may include No pointing, gest is a characteristic finding. Screening for Signs of Autism Preliminary symptoms may include No pointing, gest... (Slide 8)

scenarios.rationale: s involved in, such behavior Expectations must be in line with the chi is a known risk factor. Guidance and Discipline Goal is to teach,  not  punish Discipline involves guida... (Slide 9)

scenarios.question: A toddler's favorite word is often 'no' because of which developmental task?
scenarios.rationale: Erikson's Autonomy vs Shame/Doubt (1-3 years) explains negativism. Toddlers assert independence through 'no'. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Toilet training readiness includes:
options: Ability to sit, stay dry 2 hours, communicate need, follow simple directions
scenarios.rationale: Readiness signs: sphincter control, dry periods, communication, interest in toileting, ability to follow directions. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Which is an appropriate discipline strategy for a toddler?
options: Time-out for 1 minute per year of age
scenarios.rationale: Time-out: 1 minute per year of age (max 3 min). Effective when used consistently and calmly. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A 2-year-old should have vocabulary of approximately:
scenarios.rationale: 2-year-olds: ~50-300 words, 2-word phrases. 3-year-olds: ~1000 words, 3-word sentences. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Parallel play is characteristic of which age group?
scenarios.rationale: Parallel play: toddlers play alongside but not with each other. Cooperative play develops in preschool. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Temper tantrums in toddlers are:
scenarios.rationale: Tantrums are normal in toddlers as they learn emotional regulation. Respond calmly, ensure safety, ignore attention-seeking. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Which safety measure is MOST important for toddlers?
options: Childproofing the home and close supervision
scenarios.rationale: Toddlers explore actively. Childproofing (locks, gates, outlet covers) + constant supervision prevents injuries. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A toddler who can stack 6-7 blocks demonstrates:
scenarios.rationale: Stacking blocks: 6 blocks by 24 months. Shows hand-eye coordination and spatial awareness. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: What is the primary nutrition concern for toddlers?
options: Iron deficiency anemia from excessive milk intake
scenarios.rationale: Too much milk (>24 oz/day) displaces iron-rich foods → milk anemia. Limit to 16-24 oz/day. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A 2.5-year-old who wets the bed at night:
scenarios.rationale: Nighttime dryness may not occur until age 5-7. Enuresis before age 5 is normal. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A toddler's normal pulse rate is:
scenarios.rationale: Toddler HR: 80-130 bpm. Lower than infants. Count apical for 1 full minute. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: What is the expected weight gain for toddlers per year?
scenarios.rationale: Toddlers gain 4-6 pounds and 2-3 inches per year. Growth velocity slows compared to infancy. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A 3-year-old can jump in place. This is:
scenarios.rationale: Jumping in place: 24-30 months. Hopping on one foot: 4 years. Skipping: 5 years. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: Which is appropriate discipline for a 2-year-old?
options: Taking away privileges for a week
scenarios.rationale: Toddlers: short attention span. Redirection/distraction works best. Time-out also effective. (Standard NCLEX)

scenarios.question: A toddler who says 'me do it' is demonstrating:
scenarios.rationale: Erikson's Autonomy: toddlers want to do things themselves. Encourage safe independence. (Standard NCLEX)
